{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=draft}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} Western Court

Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine}}

Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi|12=draft}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusuhi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi|12=draft}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|12=draft}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine}}

Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine[[Category:qq]] }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=draft|12=draft}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine[[Category:qq]] }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]

(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusuhi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine[[Category:qq]] }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==

The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==

In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusuhi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōtango|qq=}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōtango|qq=}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province|qq=}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/modern shrine Ranking system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] |- |Second Hall||[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||[[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and eventually a shrine during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. === Modern Period === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]]

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and eventually a shrine during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. === Modern Period === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Main hall
(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] |- |Second Hall||[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||[[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] |}

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and eventually a shrine during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. === Modern Period === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]]

Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] |- |Second Hall||[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||[[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and eventually a shrine during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. === Modern Period === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]]

(With stone lanterns, Important Cultural Properties, in front) |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] |- |Second Hall||[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||[[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] |}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] |- |Second Hall||[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||[[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and eventually a shrine during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. === Modern Period === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyanome|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Historic Sites]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[en:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]]
