revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434413]] Wikidata name: Ōmiyame-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434413]] Wikidata name: Ōmiyame-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} Western Court

The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} Western Court

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja]][[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:大宮売神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōmiyame Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|大宮町周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}} |Latitude = 35.35.28.37 |Longitude = 135.6.6.73 |ISO = JP-26 |deity = {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}
Wakamiya-no-Kami |ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} 2 seats)
(Traditionally) Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |AnnualFestival = October 10 |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Othernames = Ōmiyame Shrine・Suki Shrine・Suki Palace |LabelPosition = top |Map = Japan Kyoto |location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.591111|N|135.101944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Omiyame-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Ōmiyame Shrine''' (おおみやめじんじゃ/おおみやのめじんじゃ, '''Ōmiyame Shrine''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Suki|ja|周枳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suki|1=Ōmiya-chō Suki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112082646}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is traditionally known as the Second Shrine of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Historically, it was also referred to as "Suki Shrine" or "Suki Palace." == Overview == Ōmiyame Shrine is located on a terrace on the west slope of Mount Kizumi, upstream of the Takenogawa River in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Its deities are {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} and Wakamiya-no-Kami. It is the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} to enshrine Ōmiyame-no-Kami outside the Imperial Court, and it is sometimes considered the original shrine of Ōmiyame-no-Kami worshipped at the court. The shrine precinct is also an archaeological site, yielding numerous artifacts from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} onward. Notably, mid-Kofun period ritual artifacts have been discovered, confirming the site's development from an ancient ritual ground into a Shinto shrine. In addition to the shrine's unique deity and its transition from primitive to Ritsuryō-period worship, the site is of great historical and archaeological significance. The precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1981. Two stone lanterns are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, and the shrine's old main hall is a Tangō City-designated Tangible Cultural Property. Local performing arts, such as the Three Role Dance, Sasa-bayashi, and Kagura, were also designated as Intangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture in 1981 and of Kyōtango City the same year. == Deities ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} Western Court
{{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} Deities !Hall!!deity |- |First Hall||{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}} |- |Second Hall||{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}} |- |Third Hall||Tama-tsumu-musuhi |- |Fourth Hall||Ikumusubi |- |Fifth Hall||Ashimusubi |- |Sixth Hall||{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} |- |Seventh Hall||Miketsu |- |Eighth Hall||{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |}
The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesShrine pamphlet.: * '''{{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|Ōmiyame-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}}''' * '''Wakamiya-no-Kami''' The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} lists two seats for Ōmiyame Shrine, and one of the deities, Ōmiyame-no-Kami, is well-known for being enshrined at the Imperial Court as one of the Eight Hasshinden Deities. No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:大宮売神社]] [[ja:Ōmiya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

No other shrine bearing the name Ōmiyame exists, making this the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} enshrining the deity outside the court. == History == === Establishment === The exact establishment of the shrine is unknownŌmiyame Shrine, Heibonsha, 1981.. Archaeological evidence suggests the area was a settlement from the Yayoi period, later becoming a ritual site during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} and eventually a shrine during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. === Modern Period === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a Village Shrine in 1873 and later promoted to Prefectural Shrine in 1924. In 1986, the precinct was designated a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Two stone lanterns (Kamakura period) === Kyoto Prefectural Designations === * Historic Site: Shrine precinct (designated 1986) === Kyōtango City Designations === * Tangible Cultural Property: Former Main Hall (Edo period, 1695) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|オオミヤノメ|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} * {{ill|Hasshinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/370401.html Ōmiyame Shrine] - Kokugakuin University COE Program: Shinto and Shrine Historical Archives. {{translated page|ja|大宮売神社|version=97973936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Omiyame-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434413|co|commons:Category:Omiyame-jinja|ja|大宮売神社|ja|Ōmiya Shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
